Efreeti Ice Staff  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 2H Blunt Atk Delay: 35
DMG: 30 Dmg Bonus: 130 AC: 10
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
Effect: Frostbite (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 50
WT: 4.5 Size: TINY
Class: MNK
Race: HUM IKS FRG DRK
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

▲ Page top
nice staff
QuoteReply
# Jun 03 2002 at 3:36 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591775369747
2 posts
I just sold this for 11k on bristlebane. It is very nice. I have never had a big problem with agro from the proc. The one time I was having too much agro, I switched back to fist and goranga club and got the same agro because my group sucked.
Bottom line is that if you're gaining a ton of agro with this weapon, than your tank isn't much of a tank.
I sold the weapon because I want to start soloing more and wanted a stun weapon.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 18 2002 at 7:59 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) If u drag away aggro from the main tank, the you suck :)
RE: nice staff
QuoteReply
# Jun 27 2002 at 7:23 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757459116
13 posts
I use this Efreeti Ice Staff and it's proc is not a monk killer for me at all. But then I'm a 57 monk with epic, so I'm going to out taunt a low level tank class anyway. But in a group of peers, this weapon is no less safe to use than any others.

I feel the problem with the term 'monk killing' comes from when payers twink their monk with weapons well above their level. If you do that, then you will run the risk of out taunting the not so twinked warrior. But that's a problem you are going to have to deal with as a price for twinking your monk.

As a side note, in the Kael Arena I have found that named giants tend to dislike its proc, so hit me a few times before returning to the tank. But so far in other zones, it's taunt is easy to manage.

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: May 21 2002 at 1:35 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) just have to say...
Warriors vs Monks
QuoteReply
# Mar 05 2002 at 5:12 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1595175385077
23 posts
This is a bit out of topic but I have seen so many posts on Warriors vs Monks that I felt I just had to chime in.

There really is no conflict or comparison. I am a monk and when I am in a raid the big warrior tank is my best friend. Often I pull and he peels. I let him gain agro and then smash the mobs. We are constantly in communications during pulls, while OTM, discusing mobs and how many we can handle. We both have very important roles to play and from my experience we have a lot of respect for each other.

In terms of weapons and gear, I am the first one to say, hey the warrior needs that hi AC/HP gear or we are all going to die. Give him the hi haste item so he can easily hold taunt. And you know what, the big warriors always seem to watch my back while i am away pulling and if something I can use drops they stick up for me to ensure i have a fair shot at it.

In terms of the quality of gear, it consistent with verants vision as having Wariors as Tanks and Monks as pullers and best toe to toe dammage dealers (Yes, rogues do more dammage from the rear, no flames needed, hehe).

Ok, its off my chest and I feel better now. Thanks for putting up with me.

Shuyung Softfist
54th Epic Monk
Agema
▲ Page top
RE: Warriors vs Monks
QuoteReply
# Apr 07 2003 at 12:46 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591787392908
2 posts
Woot!

I know this was posted almost a full year ago, but I must commend you on a truly wonderful picture of how smart players and good grouping situations really work. It's really refreshing hearing about how well different classes complement each other rather than the constant drone of folks trying to get a quick ego boost with talk of "my class is better than your class."
My two cents is that you can be an uber leveled character with all their AA abilities learned and all the uberest of uber equipment, but if you expect other folks to truly enjoy your company and have respect for you, you better know how your class relates to the rest of your group most effectively.
Really good to hear from you Shuyung!

Trenchent ~ 32 Enchanter
Leonax Dol'Aria ~ 36 Bard
...and many other alts ;)

▲ Page top

#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 28 2002 at 5:47 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) not worth it to get this except for the effect. peacbringer (even tho it's as ****) would out dmg this. 28,30 and 30,35 slight diff there. the bringer has only 2 less dmg and 5 less dly. the staff has only 2 more dmg and 5 more dly. so i'd go with peacebringer if yur weearing really light gear and have all the wieght to spare but other wise get this.
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 07 2002 at 2:02 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Yep, as I thought this thing is Impossible to sell.
Sellin
QuoteReply
# Jan 05 2002 at 3:40 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361862659
10 posts
well I picked up one of these recently, but need other things. It is an insane weapon ill admit that and the low weight is nice, but im debating on weather or not the price listed here is correct.
▲ Page top
Very fun staff
QuoteReply
# Dec 19 2001 at 2:57 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591767707485
5 posts
This is the coolest staff i've ever had. I change weapons at least once a month with my monk, just because there are so many cool ones for us to choose from. And from all the ones i've used, (ifs,peacebringer, SoS, Ringed mace of ykesha, jade mace, cane of harmony, runed morning star, wu/kd, nobles walking stick, moss covered twig + others) this one has been the most fun.
When i solo it procs three or more times a fight consistantly with 122 dex.
When i'm grouped I take off my dex gear putting me at 92 dex and it hardly procs at all, and aggro isnt a big deal.
I think the speed and weight on this stick are perfect, and with 10ac its like having an extra idol of the thorned. This weapon is well rounded.

Pike Razgule
51 monk
veeshan
▲ Page top
nice wep
QuoteReply
# Dec 04 2001 at 11:39 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361624532
11 posts
i cuyrrently obtained this weapon in a trade i traded my baton of royal stature for it and i think i got a great deal since if i want to use a 1hb i can use my wu trance or just fists.. this weapon does proc at level 50, and gets resisted usually only by the cold based mobs, such as spiders in velketors, <---havent tried it on much else but the frogs in sebilis dont like it =p well anyways its a great weapon 10 ac !!!!! on a weapon is awesome . i wouldnt trade this for 2 stave of shieldings, this is just to nice.

Gonoen 50th monk of Spirits of the Elite
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 20 2001 at 7:46 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) No thanks) proc would get a Monk killed in an instant. Ask any chanter how bad debuffs aggro. Id take Tstaff/Batton of faith over this anyday <not that i can get any of them =p>
#REDACTED, Posted: May 10 2002 at 6:52 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Yes it procs a Debuff, yes that debuff pisses mobs off, yes if you suck it will get you killed, yes its a fun staff to play with. =p
well
QuoteReply
# Jul 26 2001 at 3:47 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756036786
22 posts
Just for a bit of clarification it procs at 50 and does 60 dmg and then minus about 35-55 ac. It procs often for me so i only use it when soloing or on raids. The debuff part is a MAJOR tuanter. Usually when it procs once i have the mob on me. If it procs 3 times during a fight the warrior or tuanter usually doesnt get it off me. In my tinker bags i carry around AC and Resist gear and some other utilities. Just a little reminder, when equiped with this weapon make sure you have AC and hps up because the mob will get on you. It doesnt drop all the time off Dojo mustard and there are 2 forms. The monk and the warrior form that i have seen. He will drop the staff or the axe and then often 2 other items which can vary.
▲ Page top
drops of
QuoteReply
# Jul 24 2001 at 5:18 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591755245886
103 posts
Lord Djormandijier (or what ever) :-)
▲ Page top
My 2 cents
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2001 at 11:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591759540179
1 post
Hello, whats with all the non related crap, this is not a Monk message board !! Its a discussion area for the Efreeti Ice staff. Though after reading all the posts I have to admit being a bit entertained. First off I do have this staff on my level 40 Monk Twink. It does not proc yet and I am not sure when it will start. I have spoke to many people that say it is 60dd and -50ac though not sure on the ac debuff that sounds a bit too good. If you want a good well rounded weapon this is the best in the game for monks. Good ac good weight good damage not too fast and not too slow. But it has a deley under 40 so the damage cap is not as high as it would be with a slightly slower weapon. I hit for 105 max at level 40 with STR slightly over 100. But I have tried the 38/40 and I do not do as much damage with it when soloing. Because of its weight and no ac bonus on it I loose about 100ac or more using it and can not go toe to toe with a lot of blue mobs. With the Efreeti Ice staff I solo most blue stuff just fine. At level 40 i can solo all day in Overthere and get very good Xp. On a raid this item should proove to be very sweet as it will slighly debuff the mob for every ones benifit. At level 40 I can out damage any other mele class up to close to 50. Now so I don't feel left out of the heated discussion above, Monks are awsome and would never go back now, I have a 48warrior, 30ranger, 30sk and a bunch of caster classes. The monk rules on damage. In a group the monk will out damage the wizard as long as the wizaed is pacing hime self. If the waizard dumps all his mana then he would ofcourse out damage me on that fight but would loose out on the next 4 while he medded. Think about it i do over 200 damage per 3.5 sec with this weaponand my double attack and kick. thats alot of damage over a 2 min fight. Though i have to admit I was a bit surprised last night when i grouped with a 45 mage for the first time. The air pet was hitting for 44 about ever 1 sec with its double or quad hits I did not try to do the math but would say he was maybe doing as much damage as me plus the mage was nuking which is insane anyway. I would not want to try dueling a mage, as the pet and the nukes would probably be the end of me quickly so now you all can go pick on mages since they can out damage a twinked monk and the mage can me naked to do it.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 26 2001 at 11:42 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Well sure it is an extremely good weapon, but hey guess its not better than Bo Staff of Throrsamg. My master has it, 40/35 effect: hundred blows - 110dd + stun (you can stun gods with it!)
The real stats.
QuoteReply
# Oct 09 2001 at 10:54 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591764270960
19 posts
Bo Staff of Trormsong - Dmg 40, Dly 35. +10 Str +10 Agi +5.00 Wt, No effect.

Tranquil Staff - Dmg 29, Dly 30 +10 Wt- Effect 100 blows.

Both are top notch, but I would prefer the bo staff even without the effect.
▲ Page top
2hb
QuoteReply
# Jun 05 2001 at 12:32 AM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
2hb owns for higher levels. more damage, less taunt, beautiful ripostes. Even at lower levels, I've always preferred the 2hb.
▲ Page top
monks...
QuoteReply
# Jun 04 2001 at 10:40 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
monks are a very hard class to play. (i have a lvl 57 monk) i didnt have the best equipment at lvl 51 and was pulling in karnor's at RCY. i was breaking room and 2 scryers were up all i see is

Skeletal scryer begins to cast a spell
Skeletal scryer begins to cast a spell
with 2 drolvarg sentry's on me
next thing im dead
and someone said that warriors only get 300-400 hp difference? at lvl 51 i only had 1448hp and 910ac unbuffed and it is very hard to FD pull casters cuz u have to wait til they are done casting and if they are a wizard type in can be a ***** so all u warriors that are complaining that warriors are gettin screwed just start a monk and see what it is like to play them cuz u need to know how to FD pull good

57 master
▲ Page top
RE: monks...
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2001 at 9:49 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
If its yer first char, dont start a monk. Sure, they are unequalled pullers/dmg dealers. The latter being the fun part. But Feign Death pullin is a #(@ art. You have to have perfect timing. easier at lower levels, but thats when you can turn around to look first....
▲ Page top
RE: monks...
QuoteReply
# Apr 20 2002 at 11:21 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1595780129657
294 posts
Well, I've only died once from FD Pulling casters, and all the other 30 times have been because I turned the corner and said "Oh, hello Mr. Caster.. please don't ca . . ah crap."

My first char too.. you learn fast, as soon as that spell hits, FD cuz you don't want to give them time to start casting again. lol... other than that, I find that my monk is a whole lot easier than playing a warrior.
Sure, I have less HP's, gear is limited, I worry so much about my weight that I rarely ever loot (hence I have crappy equipment), and if I am the tank.. the group better be hoping that mend is up and there are no adds. lol. Other than that, I have a blast.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 07 2002 at 3:06 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Stupid first view players , get some balls try 3rd view will ya!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 04 2001 at 2:00 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Bogus thing.It would be more nice if it was 1 hb.
wep isnt all that, proc is huge taunt.
QuoteReply
# May 25 2001 at 10:46 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Haha its a monk killer wep.

Proc is the same as on Frostbringer, a dd/debuff that all our warriors love as its a great taunt. Just what monks need... more taunt... hmmmmm.

▲ Page top
not too huge of a taunt
QuoteReply
# May 25 2001 at 2:21 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592245676632
Scholar
138 posts
While in hate lastnight, I faught some of the pulls and the proc wasn't TOO much of a taunt, the mobs tended to either hit me just a few times before returning to the other tanks, or totally ignore me, but in bigger encounters(vs gods and dragons and such) I'll probably put it away just to be safe ;)
in any event, the 10AC is nice for pulling(no, I don't have a rainbow shield, so don't suggest I use one:p)

something to keep in mind about the Frostbringer, is that it's 12/22.. speed is a good taunt... also the the warriors are using their taunt button on top of the proc on the weapon, which together, is a very nice taunt indeed, but the proc alone isn't the taunt to end all taunts
▲ Page top
How it looks
QuoteReply
# May 08 2001 at 5:03 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592245676632
Scholar
138 posts
the staff looks like the wizard staff of the wheel, or a water crystal staff, with the clear part at the top, very unique look for a monk, toss on my robe of the lost circle and can get mistaken for a wizard =)
▲ Page top
what the proc actually does
QuoteReply
# May 08 2001 at 4:58 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592245676632
Scholar
138 posts
I have one of these on my monk, the proc does 60 damage, there is no dot to it, I attacked a pretty low level mob, as soon as it proced I turned off attack.. and the life bar didn't go down at all :p
also, looking at the spell data file, it says the proc does a 60 DD and neg 15 AC, the ac debuff lasts for 1 min(60 seconds)
it's ice based, and while fighting in kedge, it seemed to go off quite a bit.. the "Your frostbite spell was resisted" over and over in red kinda stuck out :p

I haven't faught much with it other than in kedge(where things are pretty much imune to cold anyways) so far no low level mobs outside of kedge have resisted it :)
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 01 2001 at 10:31 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) ok how'd we get from auguing about a staff to what class is better. i mean come on you guys. this game is supposed to be fun. (with the exeption on dieing alot) i play nether monk nor war, so back talk if you want but it'll do you no good.
proc
QuoteReply
# Feb 25 2001 at 3:50 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
wat is the proc?? i hear
-15ac 60+dd
-50ac 60+dd
-50ac 500 dot??
or wats the real proc anyways??
▲ Page top
RE: proc
QuoteReply
# Jan 20 2002 at 6:02 PM Rating: Decent
Yrys
Scholar
36 posts
Frostbite
Decrease Current Hitpoints by 60 (L1)
Decrease Armor Class (AC) by 15 (L1)


--------------------------------------------------
Classes: None

--------------------------------------------------
Skill: Evocation
Allowable Targets: All

--------------------------------------------------
Range to Target: 200 feet

--------------------------------------------------
Resistance Check: Cold

--------------------------------------------------
Spell Duration: 10 ticks (60 seconds)
Duration Formula: 1

--------------------------------------------------
Spell cast on you: Bitter cold blasts your skin.
Spell cast on someone: Soandso is blasted by a gust of bitter cold.

--------------------------------------------------
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Feb 27 2001 at 1:41 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) its -50ac 150dd and 500 dot
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 30 2001 at 8:47 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) what the hell..did you pull those stats out of your *** or what?
#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 29 2001 at 4:49 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) The DD is only 60, i don't know if there is anything else on it. I only borrowed if for a little while :(
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 05 2001 at 6:02 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Well, only -40 ac pretty much because the staff itself adds 10ac
New monk weap
QuoteReply
# Feb 22 2001 at 10:24 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591749817370
Scholar
397 posts
Maybe some of you loser lvl 60 monks will drop them heavy *** tranquil staff and pick up one of these so you can actually be more effective overall. You might lose 100 damage a minute with this thing (feign), but hella worth it for the weight and ac, so you can load up on other good heavy monk stuff like idol of thorned or kromrif legs.

I give this staff a thumbs up... good job verant. =)

As far as the verant monk jealousy post I'd have to say, look how many warrior, sham, and druid items, were added with velious... then look at monk stuff... monk get the least amount of stuff, and are still forced to either endure the boring as hell quests or use all / all loot.

On mobs like dain, etc... monk still get owned compared to warriors who can get cobalt n stuff and actually survive a few rounds, so if this staff keeps a monk alive another round, I say "cheer"

We all know the trakanon staff is better, but this still rocks.
▲ Page top
RE: New monk weap
QuoteReply
# Mar 02 2001 at 4:14 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591754082078
2 posts
What trakanon staff?

Don't forget warriors get defensive and there is no comparison as to who does the tanking and who does the damage.

Plus, from the time you start a monk you know that you won't be needing much equipment. That explains verant not giving monks much gear (armor). In fact us monks should be glad that we get what we do and aren't still wearing shivercrap. The one thing we should get are the best weapons in the game and this is an example.

Ariesto Schmo - 59 masta tarew marr
